header.description: Networking Equipment Selector knowledge base for the Cisco Catalyst 9200 Series switches. Carries the catalog (atomic SKU facts), groups (named shared compatibility sets), and switch models for C9200 (modular uplink), C9200L (fixed uplink), C9200CX (compact, fanless, fixed uplink), C9200L-M (Meraki-managed, fixed uplink), and C9200CX-M (Meraki-managed, compact). C9200L-M model entries (14) and C9200CX-M model entries (7) are complete. Structure: catalog + groups + models.

sources.name: Cisco Catalyst 9200 Series Switches Data Sheet

design_notes: Pass 1 of 2: catalog + groups only. 'models' is empty until Pass 2; the per-model validator checks (valid_primary subset, poe_budget_matrix max, access-port sum == total_port_count) therefore do not yet run. The standalone group-integrity and catalog port-speed checks DO run.

Scope: C9200 (modular uplink, FRU PSU + FRU fans, StackWise-160) and C9200L (fixed uplink, FRU PSU + fixed fans, StackWise-80). C9200CX (compact, fanless, fixed internal PSU, UPOE/UPOE+/HVDC) is deferred to its own pass.

Meraki C9200L-M: catalog SKUs and groups added (Pass 1 of 2 for this sub-family) from the Meraki C9200L-M datasheet — 3 -M power supplies (PWR-C5-*WAC-M), 3 -M stack cables (STACK-T4-*-M) plus stack kit C9200L-STAK-KIT-M, 12 meraki-classic per-term license SKUs (LIC-C9200L-{24,48}{E,A}-{1,3,5}Y), 4 meraki-subscription tier-split license SKUs (LIC-MS-200-{M,L}-{E,A}), and the corresponding c9200l-m-psus / c9200l-m-stack-cables / 8 cat9200l-meraki-* license_groups. Two modeling decisions were made because the datasheet gives only ONE base order code per tier/port-class (no per-term or per-tier SKUs printed): (1) tier vocabulary mapped Enterprise(-E)->essentials, Advanced(-A)->advantage, consistent with the -E/-A convention used elsewhere in this file; (2) classic per-term SKUs (-1Y/-3Y/-5Y) and subscription per-tier SKUs (-E/-A) were synthesized by appending suffixes to the datasheet's base order code, mirroring the C9300L-M LIC-C9300-24E-1Y.../LIC-MS-300-M-E/-A pattern — these synthesized SKU strings are not independently datasheet-confirmed order codes. C9200L-M switch model[] entries added (Pass 2 for this sub-family, 14 models: 24T-4G/24P-4G/48T-4G/48P-4G/48PL-4G/24T-4X/24P-4X/48T-4X/48P-4X/48PL-4X/24PXG-4X/48PXG-4X/24PXG-2Y/48PXG-2Y). Every field was either directly datasheet-sourced (ports, PoE budgets, weights, power_load_watts_idle/max) or copied unchanged from the physically-identical non-M analog where the Meraki datasheet independently confirmed an identical value (switching capacity, forwarding rate, weight, PoE budget matrix) — verified line-for-line for all 14 pairs. Silicon/board-level display specs the Meraki datasheet omits (mtbf_hours, buffer_mb, uadp_asic, dram_gb, flash_gb, stack_members_max, virtual_networks, mac_address_scale) are left off entirely rather than inferred, per this file's own THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md convention that undocumented display-only attributes are simply omitted (not tracked via _incomplete, which is reserved for functional/filterable fields). ENERGY STAR certification (present on the non-M C9200L-48P-4X-E/A analog) was NOT carried over to C9200L-48P-4X-M since the Meraki datasheet makes no such claim.

Licensing: DNA-term regime only, tier locked by the -E/-A hardware suffix (Network Essentials/Advantage perpetual embedded + a mandatory-attach 3/5/7-yr DNA subscription). The newer 'Cisco Catalyst Software' (DNX) subscription printed in OG Tables 12-15 is intentionally dropped, mirroring the C9300 treatment.

Power supplies: AC only. The DC options (PWR-C5-715WDC / PWR-C6-715WDC, spare-only, PoE-models-only per datasheet Table 4) are intentionally omitted — never proposed in practice. With DC gone, each model's valid_primary is a single AC SKU (fixed by the model's port/PoE class per datasheet Table 2), and the poe_budget_matrix expresses single vs redundant (identical second PSU), not a wattage choice.
No StackPower on the C9200 series (data stacking only, StackWise-160 / StackWise-80). stackpower_cables and stackpower_cable_groups are intentionally empty.

Network-module compatibility (OG Table 3 footnotes): C9200-NM-2Y and C9200-NM-2Q are supported only on C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG; C9200-NM-4G is not supported on those PXG models. Hence two module groups (c9200-modular-uplink-modules vs c9200-pxg-uplink-modules). Fixed-uplink C9200L models carry their uplinks inline (Pass 2), so they reference no module group.

The *-NONE none-option values (C9200-SPS-NONE, C9200-STACK-NONE, etc.) are configurator constructs (CCW 'remove the category' selections), not catalog SKUs and not validated against the catalog — matching the C9300 convention. C9200-NM-BLANK is the real OG-sourced blank network module.

Series-specific groups (PSU, stack cables) are kept as their own named groups per project convention even where a SKU or cable resembles another sub-family: C9200 (Config-6 PWR-C6-*) and C9200L (Config-5 PWR-C5-*) do not share power supplies, and each uses its own stack kit (C9200-STACK-KIT vs C9200L-STACK-KIT).

C9200CX (compact) added: fixed internal power (no FRU PSU bay/redundancy) on most SKUs, represented via a synthetic single-member PSU placeholder (clearly flagged, not a Cisco SKU) so the standard configurables.power_supplies shape stays unmodified. C9200CX-12T-2X2G ships with no PSU at all (PD-powered from upstream 802.3bt Class 6/UPOE); its power_supplies group offers the two real external adapter SKUs with the 80W adapter proposed as default_primary so power is not overlooked, while the true PD-only/no-adapter case is documented (not selectable, since every catalog PSU requires watts>=1). C9200CX-8PT-2G ('Pass-through', UPOE+ redundant-uplink powered with an unsourced no-adapter PoE budget) and the datasheet-prose-only 'C9200CX-12PT-2G' (absent from every ordering/spec table, treated as a typo for 8PT-2G) are intentionally excluded from scope. No stacking on any C9200CX model.

C9200CX-M (Meraki compact) added: 7 model entries (C9200CX-12T-2X2G-M, -12P-2X2G-M, -8P-2X2G-M, -8UXG-2X-M, -12P-2XGH-M, -8P-2XGH-M, -8UXG-2XH-M), one per existing non-Meraki C9200CX-E/A hardware variant. C9200CX-8PT-2G-M is intentionally excluded — the non-Meraki C9200CX-8PT-2G base was itself excluded from scope (see above) for lack of sourced PoE budget, so there is no hardware analog to mirror. UNLIKE C9200L-M, no Cisco datasheet exists anywhere for the C9200CX Meraki variant — confirmed by the user, who also confirmed the SKUs exist in CCW. Per explicit user direction: every hardware field (ports, PoE type/budget, switching capacity, forwarding rate, weight, MTBF, PSU group/valid_primary/default_primary/poe_budget_matrix, included_by_default_non_selectable) is copied UNCHANGED from the corresponding non-Meraki C9200CX-<model>-E analog (the -E/-A pair is hardware-identical, differing only in license) on the assumption that Meraki management doesn't change the physical chassis — this is NOT independently datasheet-verified the way the C9200L-M copy was, and is asserted rather than sourced. Per confirmed user direction, this is NOT tracked via _incomplete / THINGS-TO-COMPLETE.md (unlike the MS130/MS150/C9350 gaps, which are). No new PSU/adapter catalog SKUs were created — C9200CX-M reuses the existing synthetic internal-PSU placeholders (C9200CX-PSU-315WAC-INTERNAL / -315WHVDC-INTERNAL) and the existing C9K-80W-ADPT/C9K-ADPT-DC adapters as-is, since (unlike C9200L-M's PSUs/stack cables) there is no evidence Cisco issues distinct -M PSU part numbers for CX, and C9200CX-M has no stacking (same as non-Meraki CX). NOTE: the non-Meraki C9200CX-8UXG-2XH-E/A analog's included_by_default_non_selectable.power_cables.count=1 appears inconsistent with its HVDC siblings (12P-2XGH/8P-2XGH, count=0) in the pre-existing base data; C9200CX-8UXG-2XH-M mirrors it as-is rather than silently correcting base-file data outside this task's scope. Licensing: license SKUs (LIC-C9200CX-{E,A}-{1,3,5,7}Y for meraki-classic, LIC-MS-200-S-{E,A} for meraki-subscription, 'S' = small/compact size class parallel to the existing M=24-port/L=48-port C9200L-M classes) were provided directly by the user, not datasheet-sourced (no datasheet exists). Two deliberate deviations from the C9200L-M license pattern, both confirmed by the user: (1) the classic license has NO port-class split — a single c9200cx-meraki-classic-essentials/-advantage group applies uniformly across all C9200CX-M models regardless of 8- vs 12-port, unlike C9200L-M's per-port-class (24 vs 48) split; (2) the classic term offers 1/3/5/7Y (C9200L-M's classic license only offers 1/3/5Y, no 7Y). Same subscription-license shape as C9200L-M (meraki-subscription, no term_variable, single non-termed SKU per tier). Model-level configurables.license carries no tier_locked (both tiers offered, tier is a customer choice at order time), matching the C9200L-M / project 'the -M variant is a distinct model, not a toggle' convention.

catalog._comment: Atomic SKU facts. One row per real orderable component. What a thing IS, independent of which switch uses it.

network_modules.notes: Datasheet Table 3 '2 x 25G network module'. Speed set (1/10/25G) taken from the datasheet Table 2 C9200L-*PXG-2Y '2x 1/10/25G' fixed-uplink description (same SFP28 optics). OG Table 3 footnote: supported only on C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG.

Datasheet Table 3 '4x 1G network module'. OG Table 3 footnote: NOT supported on C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG.
Datasheet Table 3 '4x 1G/10G network module'. No compatibility footnote — supported on all modular C9200 models.

stack_cables.notes: Data stack 50 cm cable, default with the stack kit (datasheet Table 6). SKU name and datasheet say T4; OG Table 4 description column labels it 'Type 3 Stacking Cable' — nomenclature discrepancy, stored per the SKU/datasheet as type-4.

stack_kits.notes: StackWise-160 data-stack adapter kit for C9200 modular models (two data stack adapters + one 50 cm cable). Prerequisite for stacking; ships with STACK-T4-50CM (datasheet Tables 5-6).

groups._comment: Named shared compatibility SETS. A switch references a group by id and inherits the whole set.

network_module_groups.notes: Non-PXG modular C9200 models (24T, 24P, 24PB, 48T, 48P, 48PL, 48PB). C9200-NM-2Y/2Q are PXG-only and excluded; C9200-NM-4G is not on the PXG models (OG Table 3 footnotes).
C9200-24PXG / C9200-48PXG only. Adds the PXG-only 25G (NM-2Y) and 40G (NM-2Q) modules; C9200-NM-4G is not supported on PXG models (OG Table 3 footnotes).

power_supply_groups.notes: Cisco Catalyst 9200 (modular uplink) Config-6 AC PSU family (OG Table 5). AC only (DC PWR-C6-715WDC intentionally omitted). Which member is a valid PRIMARY, the default shipped PSU, and the PoE budget per (primary,secondary) pair are switch-specific and live on the model entry (Pass 2).

Cisco Catalyst 9200L (fixed uplink) Config-5 AC PSU family (OG Table 5). AC only (DC PWR-C5-715WDC intentionally omitted). Distinct SKUs from the C9200 Config-6 family (shallower chassis); kept as its own named group. Per-model valid_primary/default/PoE budget live on the model entry (Pass 2).

Fixed internal 315W AC supply on C9200CX-12P-2X2G / -8P-2X2G / -8UXG-2X. Single-member group: this chassis has no PSU bay, no field-replaceable unit, and no redundancy pair — the group exists only so the standard power_supplies shape applies.
Fixed internal 315W dual-input HVDC/AC supply on C9200CX-12P-2XGH / -8P-2XGH / -8UXG-2XH. Single-member group, same rationale as c9200cx-ac-internal-psus.

C9200CX-12T-2X2G only. This model ships with NO internal power supply (OG: 'does not come with a power supply by default'); its true default powering is as a PD drawing 802.3bt Class 6 / UPOE from an upstream switch's uplink. These two SKUs are the optional external adapters (AC-DC and DC-DC, both 80W). default_primary proposes the AC-DC adapter so power is not overlooked at order time; running with no adapter at all (pure PD) is fully valid but is not offered as a selectable group member since every catalog power_supply requires watts>=1 — see the model's axis_value_provenance.power_source.

Cisco Catalyst 9200L-M (Meraki) series PSU set (Optional Accessories table, C9200L-M Datasheet). -M SKUs, same wattages as the base C9200L Config-5 family; reuses the existing C9200L-SPS-NONE none-option per the C9300L-M convention. Which member is valid as PRIMARY and the PoE budget per (primary,secondary) pair are switch-specific and live on the model entry (Part 2).

stack_cable_groups.notes: StackWise-160 data stacking for C9200 modular models via C9200-STACK-KIT (two data stack adapters + one cable, 50 cm default). Type-4 data stack cables (datasheet Tables 5-6).

StackWise-80 data stacking for C9200L fixed-uplink models via C9200L-STACK-KIT. Same Type-4 STACK-T4 cables as the C9200 group but a distinct kit; kept as its own named group per project convention (datasheet Tables 5-6).

C9200L-M (Meraki) fixed-uplink StackWise-80 stacking via C9200L-STAK-KIT-M (two adapters + one 50cm cable, Optional Accessories table, C9200L-M Datasheet). Type-4 cables, -M SKUs; reuses the existing C9200L-STACK-NONE none-option per the C9300L-M convention. Kept distinct from c9200l-stack-cables per project convention (series-specific groups kept separate even where cable specs match).
